mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust labor market and strong underlying economic activity, which typically translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong employment growth supports consumer spending
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows consistent growth but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are predominantly negative over a 3-year period, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A significant rise in the unemployment rate above a critical threshold signals a deteriorating labor market and potential recessionary pressures, leading to a decline in corporate profits and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical correlation between rising unemployment and recessions
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises above 5.5% and S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of such instances over a 5-year period, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales combined with moderate, stable inflation indicate healthy consumer demand without overheating, creating an ideal environment for corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is a major component of GDP
failure_condition: If RSAFS shows consistent growth and CPIAUCSL remains below 3% year-over-year, but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of observations, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary policy, which raises borrowing costs for businesses and consumers, dampening economic activity and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Central bank rate hikes historically precede market corrections
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.25% over 3 months, and S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 30% of such instances over a 5-year period, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts below a key threshold indicates a weakening housing market, which can spill over into broader economic slowdowns due to reduced construction activity and consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market health is a leading economic indicator
failure_condition: If HOUST falls below 1000k units, and S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 45% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing and mining sectors, signaling strong supply-side activity and demand for goods, which supports corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production reflects real economic output
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows 3-month average growth above 0.5% but S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 2-year period, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong growth in GDP and non-farm payrolls signifies a robust and expanding economy, indicating high aggregate demand and employment, which drives corporate profitability and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP and employment are key measures of economic health
failure_condition: If both GDPC1 and PAYEMS show sustained positive growth, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of observations over a 5-year period, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: High and accelerating consumer price inflation erodes purchasing power, increases input costs for businesses, and often prompts tighter monetary policy, all of which are detrimental to corporate earnings and lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:High inflation can lead to interest rate hikes
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL 12-month change exceeds 4% and S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window, this hypothesis is falsified.
